notes.anchor: your justice in the administration of the province
notes.note: Plancus was governing Transalpine Gaul (Gallia Comata) as proconsul in 44 BC, with the founding of colonies such as Lugdunum (Lyon) among his tasks.

notes.anchor: men thought you too compliant with the times
notes.note: A delicate allusion to Plancus's earlier accommodation with Antony and the Caesarian leadership; Cicero excuses it as prudent calculation under constraint rather than genuine endorsement.
As consul-designate for 42 BC, Plancus held a recognised rank and a free hand that (Cicero argues) freed him to act on his own judgement rather than bend to the dominant faction.
